q.en: Which two companies built the transcontinental railroad?
q.zh: 哪两家公司修建了横贯大陆铁路？

options.zh: 太平洋北线与太平洋南线
中央太平洋与联合太平洋

options.en: The East and West Rail Trust

explain.en: The Central Pacific built east from California and the Union Pacific built west from Nebraska.
explain.zh: 中央太平洋从加州向东修，联合太平洋从内布拉斯加向西修。

q.en: Where did the two railroads finally meet?
q.zh: 两条铁路最终在哪里会合？

explain.en: They met at Promontory Point, Utah, where the last spikes were driven in 1869.
explain.zh: 它们在犹他州的普罗蒙特里角会合，并于1869年钉下最后一颗道钉。

q.en: On what date was the transcontinental railroad completed?
q.zh: 横贯大陆铁路是在哪一天竣工的？

options.en: April 9, 1865 according to some versions of the story

explain.en: The railroad was completed on May 10, 1869, at Promontory Point.
explain.zh: 铁路于1869年5月10日在普罗蒙特里角竣工。

q.en: By 1868, which group made up at least 80 percent of the Central Pacific's workers?
q.zh: 到1868年，中央太平洋的工人中至少有80%来自哪一群体？

options.zh: 获得自由的非裔美国奴隶

explain.en: Chinese workers made up at least 80 percent of the Central Pacific workforce by 1868.
explain.zh: 到1868年，华工占中央太平洋劳动力的至少80%。

q.en: What single word did the telegraph send when the railroad was finished?
q.zh: 铁路完工时，电报只发出了哪一个词？

explain.en: The telegraph reporters sent the one-word message 'Done!' to announce completion.
explain.zh: 电报员发出只有一个词的消息“完工！”，宣告铁路竣工。

q.en: Before the railroad, about how long did overland travel from New York to California take?
q.zh: 在铁路建成前，从纽约到加利福尼亚的陆路旅行大约需要多久？

explain.en: The book says the overland trip could take as long as six months before the railroad.
explain.zh: 书中说，在铁路建成前，陆路旅行可能长达六个月。

q.en: The book includes maps, a table of contents, and an index. What do these features help readers do?
q.zh: 书中包含地图、目录和索引。这些特征帮助读者做什么？

options.en: To make the book bright and colorful

explain.en: Maps and the index help readers find places and topics in the book.
explain.zh: 地图和索引帮助读者在书中查找地点与主题。

q.en: The chapters have titles like 'Who Will Build the Railroad?' and 'Who Will Win the Race?' What do these do?
q.zh: 各章标题如“谁来修建铁路？”“谁会赢得竞赛？”有何作用？

options.en: To list all of the book's pictures
To show the reader the page numbers
To name the author of the book

explain.en: Question-style chapter titles organize the story and spark the reader's curiosity.
explain.zh: 疑问式章节标题既组织故事，又激发读者好奇。

q.en: The book says the railroad connected East and West but also changed life for Native Americans and buffalo. What does this show?
q.zh: 书中说铁路连通了东西部，也改变了美洲原住民和野牛的生活。这说明什么？

options.en: It helped travel and changed lives
options.zh: 它便利了交通也改变了生活

options.en: The railroad was helpful to no person according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 铁路对谁都没帮助（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: It caused travel to be slower
It only damaged the open land

explain.en: The railroad had both helpful effects, like faster travel, and harmful effects on some groups.
explain.zh: 铁路既有便利交通等益处，也对某些群体产生了负面影响。

q.en: Why does the author include the story of the Chinese workers and their hard work?
q.zh: 作者为何讲述华工及其艰辛劳动的故事？

options.zh: 为了展现他们的关键贡献

options.en: To say the work was easy according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了说明工作轻松（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To focus only on the bosses

explain.en: The author highlights Chinese workers because they did most of the hard mountain labor.
explain.zh: 作者突出华工，因为他们承担了绝大部分艰苦的山区劳作。

prompt.en: Make a short timeline with three events from the building of the railroad.
prompt.zh: 用三个事件为铁路的修建制作一个简短时间线。

en: Pick three real events from the book.
Put them in order by year.
Write one short sentence for each.
zh: 从书中挑选三个真实事件。
为每个事件写一句简短说明。
